title: Complete Documentation & Cyber Services Page - Frontend Implementation

requirements.summary: Create DocumentationPage component with hero section, CTAs, and trust badges
acceptanceCriteria: Page displays professional hero section with title 'Online Documentation & Cyber Services' and subtitle
Three CTA buttons present: 'Apply Online', 'Visit Our Centre', 'Chat on WhatsApp'
Trust badges displayed: Fast Service, Secure Documents, Trusted Support
All sections render correctly on desktop and mobile

file_operations.description: Create new DocumentationPage component with hero section displaying title 'Online Documentation & Cyber Services', subtitle 'Apply for government documents, exam forms, and digital services from home or visit our center for assistance', three CTA buttons (Apply Online scrolls to form, Visit Our Centre links to contact, Chat on WhatsApp opens WhatsApp), and trust badges section with icons for Fast Service, Secure Documents, and Trusted Support. Use StudyNeeds primary blue (#2563eb) for headers and CTAs, secondary orange (#f97316) for accents. Ensure responsive layout for mobile devices.

requirements.summary: Implement four service category sections with detailed service cards
acceptanceCriteria: Four distinct service category cards displayed with appropriate icons
All services listed under correct categories
Note about Aadhaar biometric requiring centre visit is visible
Cards are responsive and styled with StudyNeeds theme colors

file_operations.description: Add four service category sections to DocumentationPage: 1) Government Certificate Services (Caste Certificate, Income Certificate, Residence Certificate, Birth Certificate, E-Shram Card, Labour Card, Ayushman Card, Ration Card), 2) ID & Personal Document Services (PAN Card, Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Passport Apply, PVC Card with prominent note 'Aadhaar Biometric Service requires centre visit'), 3) Job, Exam & Education Services (Government Job Forms, Admit Card Download, Result Check, Scholarship Forms, College Admission Forms, Online Counselling), 4) Cyber Cafe & Utility Services (Train/Bus Tickets, Bill Payment, Mobile Recharge, Resume Making, Job Applications, Print/Scan/Lamination, Passport Size Photo). Use card layout with icons, organized in responsive grid using StudyNeeds theme colors.

requirements.summary: Create Online vs Centre Visit comparison section
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison table or two-column layout clearly shows online vs centre services
All services listed in correct categories
Section is visually distinct and easy to understand
Responsive layout for mobile devices

file_operations.description: Add comparison section with two columns to DocumentationPage. Left column 'Services Possible From Home' lists: PAN Card Apply, Certificate Applications, Government Job Forms, Scholarship Forms, Admit Card Download, Result Check, Bill Payment, Resume Making, Ticket Booking. Right column 'Services Requiring Centre Visit' lists: Aadhaar Biometric Update, Passport Size Photo, Document Scanning, PVC Card Printing, Lamination Services. Use table or two-column card layout with checkmark icons for online services and location pin icons for centre services. Ensure mobile-responsive stacked layout.

requirements.summary: Implement pricing section with service charges and disclaimer
acceptanceCriteria: All pricing items displayed with correct amounts
Government fees disclaimer note is prominently visible
Pricing section uses cards or table format
Mobile responsive layout

file_operations.description: Add pricing section to DocumentationPage displaying service charges in card format: Form Filling (₹50 onwards), Certificate Applications (₹100 onwards), PAN Card Apply (₹150 onwards), Scholarship Forms (₹100 onwards), Resume Making (₹99 onwards), Printing Services (₹5 per page). Include prominent disclaimer box stating 'Government fees are separate and payable as per official rules' styled with orange accent background. Use grid layout for pricing cards with rupee symbol and 'onwards' text. Ensure mobile-responsive design.

requirements.summary: Create service request form component with all required fields
acceptanceCriteria: All form fields present with correct input types
Service dropdown populated with relevant service options
Mode selection allows choosing between online and centre visit
File upload field accepts multiple document uploads
Form validates required fields before submission
Success message displays after submission: 'Our team will contact you shortly'

file_operations.description: Create DocumentationServiceForm component with fields: Full Name (text input, required), Mobile Number (tel input, 10 digits, required), Service Needed (dropdown with options from all service categories listed in REQ-2, required), Mode (radio buttons: 'Online Service' or 'Visit Centre', required), Upload Documents (file input with multiple file support, accept common document formats), Message (textarea, optional). Add Submit Request button. Implement client-side validation for required fields and phone number format. Use Tailwind styling with StudyNeeds colors. Form will be wired to backend mutation in REQ-11.

Import and render DocumentationServiceForm component in DocumentationPage within a dedicated 'Request a Service' section. Add section heading and brief instruction text. Position form prominently after service categories and before How It Works section.

requirements.summary: Create React Query hooks for documentation request operations
acceptanceCriteria: All four hooks implemented following existing query hook patterns
Mutation hooks show toast notifications on success/error
Query invalidation configured for mutations
Hooks use useActor to get backend actor instance

file_operations.description: Add four React Query hooks to existing useQueries.ts file following the established patterns: 1) useCreateDocumentationRequest mutation hook that calls actor.createDocumentationRequest with parameters (fullName, mobileNumber, serviceNeeded, mode, uploadedDocuments as ExternalBlob array, message), shows success toast 'Request submitted successfully', invalidates 'documentationRequests' query key on success. 2) useGetDocumentationRequests query hook that calls actor.getDocumentationRequests, queryKey 'documentationRequests', enabled when actor exists. 3) useUpdateDocumentationRequestStatus mutation hook that calls actor.updateDocumentationRequestStatus with (requestId, newStatus), shows success toast 'Status updated', invalidates 'documentationRequests'. 4) useGetDocumentationRequestDocuments query hook that calls actor.getDocumentationRequestDocuments with requestId parameter, queryKey includes requestId, enabled when actor exists and requestId provided. Follow existing error handling and toast notification patterns from the file.

requirements.summary: Wire service request form to backend mutation with file upload handling
acceptanceCriteria: Form submission triggers mutation with all field data
Files converted to appropriate blob format for backend storage
Loading state shown during submission
Success message 'Our team will contact you shortly' displays after successful submission
Form resets after successful submission
Error messages shown if submission fails

file_operations.description: Wire DocumentationServiceForm to backend: Import useCreateDocumentationRequest mutation hook from useQueries. Import ExternalBlob from backend.d.ts. Add form state management using React useState for all fields. Implement file upload handler that converts selected files to ExternalBlob array using ExternalBlob.fromBytes() after reading files with FileReader. Wire form onSubmit to call mutation.mutate with all form data. Show loading spinner on submit button when mutation.isPending. On mutation success, display success modal or toast with message 'Our team will contact you shortly', then reset form fields. On mutation error, display error toast with error message. Add form validation before submission. Use blob-storage component's ExternalBlob class for file handling.

requirements.summary: Create DocumentationRequestsManagement admin component with table and filters
acceptanceCriteria: Component fetches and displays all documentation requests using useGetDocumentationRequests hook
Table shows all specified columns with proper formatting
Search functionality filters requests by name or mobile number
Status filter dropdown allows filtering by pending/processing/completed
Loading and error states handled appropriately

file_operations.description: Create DocumentationRequestsManagement admin component following the pattern of TuitionLeadsManagement. Use useGetDocumentationRequests hook to fetch all requests. Display table with columns: Name (fullName), Mobile (mobileNumber), Service (serviceNeeded), Mode (mode), Status (status with color badges: pending=yellow, processing=blue, completed=green), Date (formatted timestamp), Actions (View Details button). Implement search input that filters by fullName or mobileNumber. Add status filter dropdown with options: All, Pending, Processing, Completed. Include DocumentationRequestDetailModal (from REQ-13) that opens when View Details clicked, passing request data and onClose handler. Handle loading state with skeleton or spinner. Handle error state with message. Use Tailwind styling consistent with other admin management components.

requirements.summary: Create DocumentationRequestDetailModal showing complete request info with document downloads
acceptanceCriteria: Modal displays all request fields in organized layout
Uploaded documents shown with download functionality using useGetDocumentationRequestDocuments
Status dropdown allows admin to update request status
Status update triggers useUpdateDocumentationRequestStatus mutation
Modal has close button and proper styling

file_operations.description: Create DocumentationRequestDetailModal component following pattern of StudentLeadDetailModal. Accept props: request (DocumentationRequest object), onClose callback. Display all request fields in sections: Personal Details (fullName, mobileNumber), Service Information (serviceNeeded, mode), Message (message text), Status (current status with badge), Submission Date (formatted timestamp). Add Uploaded Documents section using useGetDocumentationRequestDocuments hook with request.id to fetch documents. Display document list with download buttons that use ExternalBlob.getDirectURL() for each document. Add Status Update dropdown (Pending, Processing, Completed) that calls useUpdateDocumentationRequestStatus mutation when changed. Include Close button. Use modal overlay with card styling, Tailwind classes, StudyNeeds colors. Handle loading state for documents fetch.

requirements.summary: Add Documentation Requests tab to AdminDashboardPage
acceptanceCriteria: New tab 'Documentation Requests' added to admin dashboard tabs
Tab displays DocumentationRequestsManagement component
Summary card shows count of total documentation requests
Tab follows existing admin dashboard styling and layout patterns

file_operations.description: Add 'Documentation Requests' tab to AdminDashboardPage following the existing tab pattern. Import DocumentationRequestsManagement component and useGetDocumentationRequests hook. Add tab button to the tab list. In the summary cards section at top, add a new summary card showing total count of documentation requests using documentationRequests?.length from useGetDocumentationRequests hook, with appropriate icon (use frontend/public/assets/generated/admin-orders-icon.dim_128x128.png or similar document icon). When Documentation Requests tab is active, render the DocumentationRequestsManagement component. Ensure tab styling matches existing tabs (Products, Orders, Users, Tuition Leads, Teacher Registrations).

requirements.summary: Add How It Works section with three-step process
acceptanceCriteria: Three-step process displayed in order
Each step has descriptive text matching specifications
Icons or numbers indicate step sequence
Section is visually distinct and easy to understand

file_operations.description: Add 'How It Works' section to DocumentationPage with three steps displayed in horizontal card layout (or vertical on mobile): Step 1 with icon/number - 'Submit your request online' with description 'Fill the form with your details and upload required documents', Step 2 - 'Our team verifies documents' with description 'We review your submission and confirm requirements', Step 3 - 'Service completed or visit scheduled' with description 'Get your service done online or schedule a centre visit'. Use numbered badges (1, 2, 3) or step icons. Style with card layout, blue accent for step numbers, responsive grid that stacks on mobile. Position section after service categories and before Why Choose section.

requirements.summary: Implement Why Choose The Cyber Point section with five features
acceptanceCriteria: All five features displayed with appropriate icons
Features arranged in grid or card layout
Text content matches specifications exactly
Section styled consistently with StudyNeeds theme

file_operations.description: Add 'Why Choose The Cyber Point' section to DocumentationPage with five feature cards in responsive grid layout: 1) Trusted Local Service - 'Serving the community with reliable documentation services', 2) Affordable Charges - 'Transparent pricing with no hidden costs', 3) Fast Processing - 'Quick turnaround time for all services', 4) Experienced Operators - 'Skilled team with years of experience', 5) Secure Document Handling - 'Your documents are safe and confidential with us'. Use icons for each feature (shield for trusted, rupee for affordable, clock for fast, user for experienced, lock for secure). Style with card layout in 2-3 column grid (single column on mobile), StudyNeeds blue and orange accents. Position before Terms & Conditions section.

requirements.summary: Add Terms & Conditions section with all specified terms
acceptanceCriteria: All six terms listed clearly
Terms presented in readable format (bullet points or numbered list)
Section visually separated from other content
Text matches specifications exactly

file_operations.description: Add 'Terms & Conditions' section to DocumentationPage displaying six terms in numbered or bulleted list format: 1) 'Service charges are non-refundable after submission to government portals', 2) 'Government fees are separate and payable as per official rules', 3) 'Approval of applications depends on government authority decision', 4) 'User is responsible for providing correct information and documents', 5) 'Processing time may vary based on government portal and authority', 6) 'Aadhaar biometric services require physical presence at our centre'. Use list container with border, light background, readable typography. Position near bottom of page before footer.

acceptanceCriteria: Privacy note displayed prominently
Text matches specification exactly
Positioned appropriately near forms or at page bottom

file_operations.description: Add Privacy Policy note section to DocumentationPage displaying text 'User documents are kept confidential and used only for service processing'. Style as info box with lock icon, light blue background, positioned after the service request form and before How It Works section. Use card styling with icon and centered or left-aligned text.

requirements.summary: Add Call to Action section with help buttons
acceptanceCriteria: Three CTA buttons displayed prominently
WhatsApp button opens WhatsApp Web with pre-filled message
Call Now button triggers tel: link (number to be configured)
Visit Centre button links to contact or location information
Buttons styled consistently with StudyNeeds theme

file_operations.description: Add 'Need Help?' Call to Action section to DocumentationPage near bottom before footer. Display heading 'Need Help?' with three prominent CTA buttons in horizontal layout (stacked on mobile): 1) 'Chat on WhatsApp' button (green background) - opens WhatsApp Web at https://wa.me/PHONENUMBER?text=Hello,%20I%20want%20documentation%20service%20from%20StudyNeeds (replace PHONENUMBER with configured number, default placeholder +919876543210), 2) 'Call Now' button (blue background) - tel: link (href='tel:+919876543210' as placeholder), 3) 'Visit Centre' button (orange background) - scrolls to contact/location section or opens contact info. Use button styling with icons (WhatsApp, phone, location icons). Center-align buttons with spacing.

requirements.summary: Add page-specific floating WhatsApp button to Documentation page
acceptanceCriteria: Floating button visible on Documentation page
Opens WhatsApp with specified pre-filled message
Positioned to avoid overlap with existing WhatsApp button
Button styled consistently with site design

file_operations.description: Add floating WhatsApp button to DocumentationPage component positioned in bottom-left corner (to avoid overlap with existing site-wide WhatsApp button in bottom-right from WhatsAppButton component). Create button element with WhatsApp icon, green background, fixed position, z-index for visibility. Set href to https://wa.me/PHONENUMBER?text=Hello,%20I%20want%20documentation%20service%20from%20StudyNeeds. Style with circular shape, shadow, hover effects. Position: fixed, bottom: 24px, left: 24px. Include only when viewport width allows both buttons without overlap, or hide on mobile to avoid clutter.

requirements.summary: Update ServicesSection to activate Documentation & Cyber Services card
acceptanceCriteria: Coming Soon badge removed from Documentation card
Card clickable and navigates to /documentation route
Card styling matches other active service cards
Description text reads 'Apply for government documents, exam forms, and digital services from home or visit our center for assistance' or similar

file_operations.description: Update ServicesSection component to activate the Documentation & Cyber Services card. Remove the 'Coming Soon' badge and disabled state from the Documentation card. Change onClick handler to navigate to '/documentation' route using useNavigate hook. Update card description to 'Apply for government documents, exam forms, and digital services from home or visit our center for assistance'. Ensure card styling matches other active service cards (E-Commerce, Home Tuition) with hover effects, cursor pointer, and full opacity. Keep the existing icon and maintain card layout consistency.

acceptanceCriteria: Route /documentation configured in router
Route renders DocumentationPage component
Navigation from services section works correctly

file_operations.description: Add route for DocumentationPage to App.tsx router configuration. Import DocumentationPage component. Add new Route element with path='/documentation' and element={<DocumentationPage />}. Position route in the Routes list with other public pages (after tuition, before admin). Ensure route is not wrapped in AdminRoute since it's a public page. Test navigation from ServicesSection card works correctly.

requirements.summary: Apply StudyNeeds theme styling throughout Documentation page
acceptanceCriteria: Primary blue color used for main CTAs and headers
Secondary orange used for accents and highlights
White background maintained throughout
All sections responsive on mobile devices
Cards have consistent styling with shadows and borders
Icons used appropriately throughout

file_operations.description: Ensure all sections in DocumentationPage use StudyNeeds theme colors consistently: primary blue (#2563eb or bg-blue-600) for main headings, CTA buttons, section headers; secondary orange (#f97316 or bg-orange-500) for accent elements, badges, hover states, highlights; white background (bg-white) for cards and page background. Apply consistent card styling: rounded corners (rounded-lg), shadows (shadow-md on cards, shadow-lg on modals), borders (border border-gray-200). Use Tailwind responsive classes (sm:, md:, lg:) for mobile-first responsive layouts: stack cards on mobile, grid on desktop; adjust padding and font sizes for mobile. Add appropriate icons throughout using Lucide React or similar icon library (form icons, service category icons, trust badge icons, feature icons). Ensure hover effects on interactive elements. Review entire component for visual consistency with existing StudyNeeds pages (HomePage, ShopPage, TuitionPage).
